title: The Inward Room
scripture_text: The Lord is in his holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before him.
reflection: Lent is not a diet. Lent is a clearing. It is the long, slow work of making space for the only thing that satisfies. (The fourth hearing of this day.)
body:
For these forty days, something is going to be set down. It does not have to be impressive. Coffee. Sugar. Social media. The morning news. A habit of complaint.

The point is not the thing. The point is the space the thing leaves behind. That space is for God.

You will fail. You will have the coffee on day three. You will be irritated with your kids by day twelve. You will be tempted to give up the whole thing by day twenty.

Keep going. Lent is not a performance. It is a practice. The failure is part of the practice. The returning, again and again, is the point.

The Lord is waiting. He has time. He has patience. He is not grading you.

You do not have to be impressive in this moment. You do not have to be productive, or insightful, or kind of the right kind of person. The Lord of hosts, who is great in glory, has called this small, ordinary moment His own. He is in it. He is making it sing.

The world will ask you for many things. This is not one of them. The world is not watching this room. The Lord is. And He is pleased, simply, with your willingness to be here. He is pleased the way a father is pleased when a child, after a long day, comes and sits close to him and does not say anything.

Let this be the one moment today that is not for anyone. Not for the work. Not for the people. Not even for the prayer. Just for the breath. Just for the being.

In these forty days, the small acts of denying are not the point. The point is the empty space they make. The point is the small hunger that opens the heart to a larger feeding. You are not being punished. You are being uncluttered. The Lord is the one who is making the space. He has time. He will fill it.

You do not have to do anything else with this moment. You have done the work of being here. That is the whole work. Carry the stillness with you, lightly, into whatever comes next.

This is the fourth time. You have gone deeper now than you went the first time. The well is deeper. The water is still. The Lord is at the bottom. Keep going.
prayer: Lord, give me strength to set this down, and the grace to pick it up when I fail, and the wisdom to keep returning. Amen.
